Site replaced the AMR, and every AMR goes to the hospital. Resolution Details: Site removed robot 158, which was shown to not be mapped. After doing so, the ECB system was reported to be functional again. Additional Comments: Sincerely, FORTNA Technical Support Team ee

The message reports that after removing robot 158, the system began functioning again, was monitored for approximately 10 minutes, and remained operational. Useful for recovery validation, robot isolation action context, and post-action monitoring evidence.

cards.retrieval_text: Salesforce-style case screenshot for case 228723 page 12. Shows Priority 2 high production impact and subject ACB System Down. Description says zone 5 unable to get a pair, AMR had low battery under the rack, site replaced the AMR, and every AMR goes to the hospital. Resolution details say site removed robot 158 because it was not mapped, after which the ECB system was reported functional again. Signed by FORTNA Technical Support Team.

source_supported_claims.claim: The screenshot shows a priority of "2 - High: Production seriously impacted."
The screenshot subject is "ACB System Down."
The description states that under zone 5 the site was unable to get a pair.
The description mentions an AMR with a low battery level under the rack and says the site replaced the AMR.
The description says every AMR goes to the hospital.
The resolution details state that site removed robot 158, which was shown to not be mapped.
The resolution details say that after removing robot 158, the ECB system was reported to be functional again.
Stage 2 classified this artifact as a Salesforce case screenshot and hinted it as incident context evidence.

state_assessment.rationale: This is not a live operational UI, but the visible case text documents a serious production-impacting issue and also states that after removal of robot 158 the ECB system was reported to be functional again. That supports a reported recovery state rather than a directly observed healthy state.
visible_indicators: Priority is shown as high with production seriously impacted.
Subject states ACB System Down.
Resolution details say the ECB system was reported to be functional again after removing robot 158.
uncertainties: The artifact is a support-case summary, not direct system telemetry or a live status screen.
The screenshot text references both ACB System Down and ECB system functional again, so exact system naming should be reviewed by an SME.
Recovery is reported in text rather than visually demonstrated by system indicators.

candidate_support_hints.support_reason: The artifact summarizes incident symptoms and a reported corrective action, but it does not provide enough procedural detail to stand alone as a reusable runbook step.

review_uncertainty: The artifact documents reported incident details but does not directly show live system state.
The text references both ACB and ECB; this may be a source typo or differing subsystem names and should be SME-reviewed.
The phrase "unable to get a pair" is domain-specific and may need SME interpretation.
quality_notes: Artifact OCR quality is marked usable and aligns closely with the visible screenshot text.
Minor OCR noise appears at the end of the signoff line.
The screenshot is text-heavy and readable enough to support conservative extraction.

cards.short_description: Salesforce-style case text page summarizing a high-priority ACB system outage, AMR behavior, and a reported recovery action.
cards.retrieval_text: Salesforce-style incident case page for case 228723 page 12. Shows Priority 2 high production impact and subject ACB System Down. Description says zone 5 could not get a pair, an AMR had low battery under the rack, the AMR was replaced, and every AMR goes to the hospital. Resolution details say robot 158 was removed because it was not mapped, after which the ECB system was reported functional again. Signed by FORTNA Technical Support Team.

source_supported_claims.claim: The case priority is shown as '2 - High: Production seriously impacted.'
The subject shown on the page is 'ACB System Down.'
The description states the site reported under zone 5 that they were unable to get a pair.
The description states an AMR had a low battery level under the rack and that the site replaced the AMR.
The description states that every AMR goes to the hospital.
The resolution details state that site removed robot 158, which was shown to not be mapped.
The resolution details state that after removing robot 158, the ECB system was reported to be functional again.
Stage 2 classified this artifact as incident context evidence from a Salesforce case screenshot.

state_assessment.rationale: This is not a live operational UI but a case text page. The visible text reports a serious production-impacting issue and also says the system was reported functional again after a corrective action. Because the artifact records both outage and reported restoration in narrative form, the most conservative state assessment is recovery_in_progress with low confidence.
visible_indicators: Priority is shown as high with production seriously impacted.
Subject indicates system down.
Resolution text says the system was reported to be functional again.
context_indicators: The page combines symptom description and resolution details in one incident summary.
uncertainties: This is a case summary page, not a direct screenshot of system health indicators.
The text references both 'ACB System Down' and 'ECB system was reported to be functional again'; the naming may reflect inconsistency or OCR/source wording.
The reported recovery is narrative and not independently validated by a live status display in this artifact.

candidate_support_hints.support_reason: The artifact is primarily a case summary and reported resolution note, not a repeatable procedural screenshot or step-by-step operator workflow.

review_uncertainty: The artifact is a rendered text page rather than a native Salesforce UI capture with clearly visible field chrome.
The phrase 'every AMR goes to the hospital' is preserved from source text but may be domain-specific shorthand requiring SME interpretation.
The text references 'ACB System Down' while the resolution mentions 'ECB system'; SME review should confirm whether this is source wording, OCR ambiguity, or a true system-name distinction.
quality_notes: OCR quality is marked usable and aligns closely with the visible text.
The final OCR token 'ee' appears to be stray noise and does not affect the main incident content.
This artifact is strong for incident summary and reported resolution context, but weak for direct technical validation.

cards.short_description: Screenshot of a work order detail page shared in a Teams chat, showing an emergency mechanical breakdown ticket for the Haslet hub.
cards.retrieval_text: Teams chat screenshot from Ethan Crowder showing a work order detail page for a Fort Worth, TX Haslet hub location. The work order page shows P1 emergency priority, dispatched status, company covered yes, estimated service cost 2500 USD, and a problem description referencing UPS conveyor or sorting equipment emergency breakdown with an ACB needing repair in zone 5. Includes vendor assignment details and facility contact information. Useful as action or incident context evidence for dispatched maintenance response.

source_supported_claims.claim: The artifact includes a Teams-style message from Ethan Crowder to an internal audience dated March 17, 2026 at 4:01 PM.
The embedded screenshot shows a 'View Work Order Detail' page.
The work order location is shown as US, TX, Fort Worth.
The building field appears as 'THETH - HASLET HUB'.
The work order shows priority 'P1 - Emergency' and status 'D - Dispatched'.
The screenshot shows 'Company Covered: Yes' and an estimated service cost of 2500.00 USD.
The problem description references conveyor/sorting equipment, emergency breakdown, and an ACB needing repair in zone 5.
The assignment section indicates the type is 'Vendor'.
Artifact OCR and page OCR both align with the screenshot showing a work order for an emergency mechanical issue at the Haslet hub.
Stage 2 classified this artifact as an api_client_screenshot and hinted validation evidence, but the visible image more closely resembles a work order or service management page.

state_assessment.rationale: The screenshot shows an emergency-priority work order with status 'Dispatched' and a problem description indicating an emergency breakdown requiring repair. This supports an active abnormal operational condition, but the screenshot is a work order page rather than direct system health telemetry, so confidence is moderate.
visible_indicators: Priority shown as 'P1 - Emergency'
Status shown as 'D - Dispatched'
Problem description references emergency breakdown
Problem description references repair needed in zone 5
context_indicators: The artifact is shared in an internal chat context as incident-related evidence
uncertainties: The screenshot does not directly show OptiSweep application health or API response state.
Some text is small and OCR contains errors, so exact wording of several fields is uncertain.
The work order number and vendor details are not fully reliable from OCR alone.

candidate_support_hints.support_reason: The artifact documents a specific dispatched maintenance work order but does not clearly show a reusable troubleshooting or recovery procedure.

review_uncertainty: The artifact was classified in Stage 2 as an API client screenshot, but the visible image appears to be a work order/service management page.
Several OCR-derived names, numbers, and timestamps are partially garbled and should be SME-verified.
The exact work order ID and vendor name are difficult to confirm from the provided image and OCR.
quality_notes: Artifact OCR is usable but contains notable spelling and character errors.
Page OCR quality is low and should be used only as surrounding context.
Image text is small; exact field values should be verified manually if operationally important.

downstream_usage.canonical_incident_record: Use all chunk_index entries plus selected page/artifact chunks for concise incident facts.
downstream_usage.timeline_events: Extract timeline events chunk by chunk from page_text_chunks, linking artifact cards when referenced.
downstream_usage.runbook_candidates: Use timeline events plus artifact_evidence_chunks with action, diagnostic, and validation evidence roles.
downstream_usage.draft_playbooks: Use timeline events and runbook candidates; do not treat a single incident as canonical workflow approval.
